_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split design, cross-validation, evaluation metric selection, and the conditions under which each metric is appropriate.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-K-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-006 (RM, cosine: 0.622), RM-K-007 (RM, cosine: 0.612).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.4/10 (raw 0.640). Shared vocabulary: [evaluation, frameworks, knowledge, test].

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.892 (P=0.937, R=0.852). P exceeds R by 0.085 — the DCWF element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of Test & Evaluation frameworks." and "Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.730 > STS 0.640 (gap +0.090). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [evaluation, frameworks, knowledge, test] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 22.7%. Shared tokens: [evaluation, frameworks, knowledge, test]. DCWF-only: []. WIDAI-only: [appropriate, conditions, cross, design, metric, model].
